questions.q: In which Indian city does the Victoria Memorial stand?
questions.explanation: The Victoria Memorial is one of the most famous landmarks of Kolkata, in West Bengal.

questions.q: The Victoria Memorial was built as a memorial to which British monarch?
questions.explanation: It was conceived as a memorial to Queen Victoria after her death in 1901.

questions.q: What is the primary building material of the Victoria Memorial?
questions.explanation: The memorial is built mainly of white Makrana marble, the same source used for the Taj Mahal.

questions.q: What does the Victoria Memorial function as today?
questions.explanation: The Victoria Memorial is now a museum, displaying paintings, artefacts and relics of the Raj era.

questions.q: On the bank of which river does the city of the Victoria Memorial lie?
questions.explanation: Kolkata, home of the Victoria Memorial, stands on the Hooghly River, a distributary of the Ganges.

questions.q: In which Indian state is the Victoria Memorial located?
questions.explanation: Kolkata is the capital of West Bengal, where the Victoria Memorial stands.

questions.q: What surrounds the Victoria Memorial building?
questions.explanation: The memorial is set within extensive landscaped gardens covering about 64 acres.

questions.q: The Victoria Memorial was built during which period of Indian history?
questions.explanation: It was constructed during the British colonial era, in the early 20th century.

questions.q: What colour is the Victoria Memorial most famous for being?
questions.explanation: Its gleaming white marble gives the memorial its iconic appearance.

questions.q: Which large body of water lies beside the Victoria Memorial gardens?
questions.explanation: Ornamental reflecting pools in the gardens famously mirror the white marble building.

questions.q: In which year was the Victoria Memorial formally completed and opened?
questions.explanation: Construction finished and the memorial was inaugurated in 1921.

questions.q: Who first proposed the idea of building the Victoria Memorial?
questions.explanation: Lord Curzon, the Viceroy of India, proposed the memorial soon after Queen Victoria's death in 1901.

questions.q: Who was the principal architect who designed the Victoria Memorial?
questions.explanation: Sir William Emerson, then president of the Royal Institute of British Architects, designed the memorial.

questions.q: The architectural style of the Victoria Memorial is best described as which fusion?
options: Indo-Saracenic, blending British and Mughal elements
questions.explanation: The memorial is a leading example of the Indo-Saracenic style, mixing British, Mughal, Venetian and other influences.

questions.q: The Victoria Memorial was largely funded by which means?
questions.explanation: Lord Curzon raised the funds chiefly through voluntary public subscriptions from Indians and Britons.

questions.q: Who laid the foundation stone of the Victoria Memorial in 1906?
options: The Prince of Wales (later George V)
questions.explanation: The foundation stone was laid in 1906 by the Prince of Wales, who later became King George V.

questions.q: From which famous quarry was the marble for the Victoria Memorial sourced?
questions.explanation: The white marble came from Makrana in Rajasthan, the same quarries that supplied the Taj Mahal.

questions.q: Which winged figure crowns the central dome of the Victoria Memorial?
options: A figure of Victory (the 'Angel of Victory')
questions.explanation: A bronze figure of the Angel of Victory, mounted on ball bearings to turn in the wind, tops the central dome.

questions.q: Kolkata, where the Victoria Memorial stands, served as what under the British until 1911?
questions.explanation: Calcutta (Kolkata) was the capital of British India until the capital shifted to Delhi in 1911.

questions.q: What is housed inside the Victoria Memorial's collection?
questions.explanation: The museum holds paintings, sculptures, manuscripts, weapons and other artefacts of the colonial era.

questions.q: The large green open space adjacent to the Victoria Memorial is known as what?
questions.explanation: The Victoria Memorial sits at the southern end of the Maidan, Kolkata's vast central park.

questions.q: Roughly how tall is the Victoria Memorial from ground to the top of its central dome?
questions.explanation: The memorial rises to roughly 56 metres (about 184 feet) at the top of its central dome.

questions.q: Construction of the Victoria Memorial began in which year, after the foundation stone was laid?
questions.explanation: Work began following the 1906 laying of the foundation stone by the Prince of Wales.

questions.q: Which Indian firm carried out the construction of the Victoria Memorial?
questions.explanation: The building work was executed by Martin & Co., the Calcutta firm of Sir Rajendra Nath Mookerjee.

questions.q: Approximately how many acres of gardens surround the Victoria Memorial?
questions.explanation: The memorial's landscaped gardens extend over roughly 64 acres.

questions.q: Who designed the formal gardens that surround the Victoria Memorial?
options: Lord Redesdale and Sir David Prain
questions.explanation: The gardens were laid out to designs by Lord Redesdale and Sir David Prain.

questions.q: The Angel of Victory atop the dome is mounted in a special way so that it can do what?
options: Rotate freely in the wind on bearings
questions.explanation: The 16-foot bronze Angel of Victory rests on ball bearings, allowing it to rotate with the wind like a weather vane.

questions.q: Which Mughal-era monument is most often compared to the Victoria Memorial for its white marble and domed form?
questions.explanation: Its white Makrana marble and central dome invite frequent comparison with the Taj Mahal at Agra.

questions.q: The Victoria Memorial's design also draws on the architecture of which other notable domed building?
options: St Paul's Cathedral and Mughal tombs
questions.explanation: Emerson's design fused European classical ideas, echoing domed structures, with Mughal and Indian motifs.

questions.q: Queen Victoria, to whom the memorial is dedicated, also held which title in relation to India?
questions.explanation: Queen Victoria was proclaimed Empress of India in 1877, a title commemorated by the memorial.
